seoTitle: How to Add a Page Load Animation in Webflow
metaDescription: Build a smooth page load animation in Webflow using the native interactions panel, from initial states to staggered element reveals.
intro: A well-timed page load animation can make a site feel far more polished, but it needs to be built carefully to avoid choppy layout shifts. This guide covers setting up a Page Trigger animation in Webflow's interactions panel, from hiding initial states to staggering multiple elements. It's for designers who want a cinematic entrance without writing custom JavaScript.

steps.heading: Open the Interactions panel
steps.body: In the right-hand sidebar, open the Interactions panel where Webflow's native page and element triggers live.

steps.heading: Add a Page Trigger
steps.body: Create a new Page Load trigger and start building a timeline that will run automatically when the page opens.

steps.heading: Set initial hidden states
steps.body: Select the elements you want to animate, such as headings or buttons, and assign initial values like reduced opacity and a vertical offset.

steps.heading: Build the staggered timeline
steps.body: Add each element to the timeline with a slight delay between them so items appear to reveal in sequence rather than all at once.

steps.heading: Test and clean up display states
steps.body: Preview the animation across breakpoints, apply a pointer-events-none style to any preloader element so it doesn't block clicks, and publish.

faq.question: Will a page load animation slow down my site?
faq.answer: Not if it's built with Webflow's native interactions, since they're hardware-accelerated and don't add heavy custom scripts.

faq.question: How do I stop the animation from blocking clicks?
faq.answer: Set any invisible preloader or overlay element to display none or pointer-events none once the animation finishes.

faq.question: Can I use the same animation on multiple pages?
faq.answer: Yes, but you'll need to recreate or copy the interaction on each page since page triggers are set per page in Webflow.

faq.question: Why does my animation look different on mobile?
faq.answer: Element positions and sizes change across breakpoints, so it's worth previewing the animation on smaller viewports and adjusting offsets if needed.
